title: Validating Git Ref Names with Forbidden Characters

statement.markdown: Write a function that validates a Git ref name. A valid ref name consists of uppercase and lowercase English letters, digits, and hyphens. It must not start or end with a hyphen. The function should take a string as input and return `true` if it is a valid ref name, `false` otherwise.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing a Git ref name
statement.outputFormat: A boolean value indicating whether the ref name is valid
statement.notes: The function should handle ref names with ASCII characters only.

constraints.description: The length of the input ref name

examples.explanation: A valid ref name with 4 characters
A valid ref name with a hyphen
An invalid ref name starting with a hyphen
An invalid ref name ending with a hyphen
An invalid ref name containing multiple consecutive hyphens

hints.text: Check if the ref name consists of only valid characters.
Verify that the ref name does not start or end with a hyphen.

editorial.title: Validating Git Ref Names
editorial.intuition: A Git ref name is valid if it consists of uppercase and lowercase English letters, digits, and hyphens, and does not start or end with a hyphen.
editorial.bruteForce: You can use a regular expression to match the pattern of a valid ref name.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a regular expression to match the pattern of a valid ref name, and then check if the ref name starts or ends with a hyphen.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The regular expression can be used to verify that the ref name consists of only valid characters.
